questions.question: A person crosses a 600 m long street in 5 minutes. What is his speed in km per hour?
questions.explanation: Distance = 600 m = 0.6 km. Time = 5 minutes = 5/60 hours = 1/12 hours. Speed = Distance/Time = 0.6 / (1/12) = 0.6 × 12 = 7.2 km/hr.
questions.shortcut: Speed (km/hr) = (Distance in meters / Time in minutes) × (60/1000) = (600/5) × 0.06 = 120 × 0.06 = 7.2.
questions.trap_type: Not converting units (getting 600/5 = 120 m/min and not converting to km/hr)

questions.question: An aeroplane covers a certain distance at a speed of 240 kmph in 5 hours. To cover the same distance in 1 2/3 hours, it must travel at a speed of:
questions.explanation: Distance = 240 × 5 = 1200 km. New time = 5/3 hours. New speed = 1200 / (5/3) = 1200 × 3/5 = 720 kmph.
questions.shortcut: Speed is inversely proportional to time. New speed = 240 × (5 / (5/3)) = 240 × 3 = 720.
questions.formula_used: Speed × Time = constant for same distance
questions.trap_type: Adding time difference to speed (240 + something) instead of inverse proportion

questions.question: If a person walks at 14 km/hr instead of 10 km/hr, he would have walked 20 km more. The actual distance travelled by him is:
questions.explanation: Let actual distance = x km. Time is same in both cases. So x/10 = (x+20)/14. 14x = 10x + 200. 4x = 200. x = 50 km.
questions.shortcut: Difference in distance = 20 km. Difference in speed = 4 km/hr. Time = 20/4 = 5 hours. Distance = 10 × 5 = 50 km.
questions.trap_type: Using 20 km directly with speeds (20/4 = 5, then 14×5 = 70, wrong)

questions.question: A train can travel 50% faster than a car. Both start from point A at the same time and reach point B 75 km away from A at the same time. On the way, however, the train lost about 12.5 minutes while stopping at the stations. The speed of the car is:
questions.explanation: Let car speed = x kmph. Train speed = 1.5x kmph. Time taken by car = 75/x hours. Time taken by train = 75/(1.5x) + 12.5/60 hours. Setting equal: 75/x = 75/(1.5x) + 12.5/60. 75/x = 50/x + 12.5/60. 25/x = 12.5/60. x = 25 × 60/12.5 = 120 kmph.
questions.shortcut: Time saved by train (without stops) = 75/x - 75/1.5x = 25/x hours = 12.5 minutes = 12.5/60 hours. So x = 25 × 60/12.5 = 120.
questions.formula_used: Time = Distance/Speed, accounting for stoppage time

questions.question: A bus without stopping travels at an average speed of 60 km/hr and with stoppages at an average speed of 40 km/hr. For how many minutes per hour does the bus stop?
questions.explanation: Due to stoppages, the bus covers 20 km less per hour (60-40=20). Time to cover 20 km at 60 km/hr = 20/60 hours = 1/3 hours = 20 minutes. So the bus stops for 20 minutes per hour.
questions.shortcut: Stoppage time per hour = [(Original speed - Reduced speed)/Original speed] × 60 = (20/60) × 60 = 20 minutes.
questions.formula_used: Stoppage time = [(S1 - S2)/S1] × 60 minutes per hour
questions.trap_type: Simply subtracting speeds and saying 20 minutes without proper derivation

questions.question: In a flight of 600 km, an aircraft was slowed down due to bad weather. Its average speed for the trip was reduced by 200 km/hr and the time of flight increased by 30 minutes. The duration of the flight is:
questions.explanation: Let original duration = x hours. Original speed = 600/x km/hr. New speed = 600/x - 200. New time = x + 0.5. So 600/(x+0.5) = 600/x - 200. Multiply by x(x+0.5): 600x = 600(x+0.5) - 200x(x+0.5). 600x = 600x + 300 - 200x² - 100x. 0 = 300 - 200x² - 100x. 200x² + 100x - 300 = 0. 2x² + x - 3 = 0. (2x+3)(x-1) = 0. x = 1 (positive). Duration = 1 hour.
questions.shortcut: Try options. If x=1: original speed=600, new speed=400, new time=1.5, 400×1.5=600. Matches!
questions.trap_type: Getting x=1 but then adding 0.5 to get 1.5 hours (which is new time, not original)

questions.question: A man completes a journey in 10 hours. He travels first half of the journey at the rate of 21 km/hr and second half at the rate of 24 km/hr. Find the total journey in km.
questions.explanation: Let total distance = 2x km. Time for first half = x/21. Time for second half = x/24. Total time = x/21 + x/24 = 10. x(1/21 + 1/24) = 10. x(24+21)/(21×24) = 10. x(45/504) = 10. x = 10 × 504/45 = 112. Total distance = 2x = 224 km.
questions.shortcut: Average speed for equal distances = 2ab/(a+b) = 2×21×24/45 = 22.4 km/hr. Distance = 22.4 × 10 = 224 km.
questions.trap_type: Using simple average of speeds (21+24)/2 = 22.5, then 22.5×10 = 225 (close but wrong)

questions.question: The ratio between the speeds of two trains is 7:8. If the second train runs 400 km in 4 hours, then the speed of the first train is:
questions.explanation: Speed of second train = 400/4 = 100 km/hr. Ratio 7:8 means if second is 8 parts = 100, then 1 part = 12.5. First train speed = 7 × 12.5 = 87.5 km/hr.
questions.trap_type: Calculating 400/4 = 100, then using ratio 7:8 to get 7×8=56 or 100+7=107

questions.question: A man on tour travels first 160 km at 64 km/hr and the next 160 km at 80 km/hr. The average speed for the first 320 km of the tour is:
questions.explanation: Time for first 160 km = 160/64 = 2.5 hours. Time for next 160 km = 160/80 = 2 hours. Total time = 4.5 hours. Average speed = 320/4.5 = 71.11 km/hr.
questions.trap_type: Simple average: (64+80)/2 = 72 km/hr (wrong because times are different)

questions.question: A train 240 m long passes a pole in 24 seconds. How long will it take to pass a platform 650 m long?
questions.explanation: Speed of train = 240/24 = 10 m/s. To pass platform, distance = train length + platform length = 240 + 650 = 890 m. Time = 890/10 = 89 seconds.
questions.trap_type: Using only platform length: 650/10 = 65 sec (forgetting train length)

questions.question: Two trains are moving in opposite directions at 60 km/hr and 90 km/hr. Their lengths are 1.10 km and 0.9 km respectively. The time taken by the slower train to cross the faster train in seconds is:
questions.shortcut: Relative speed = 150 km/hr = 150/3.6 = 41.67 m/s. Time = 2000/41.67 = 48 sec.
questions.formula_used: Relative speed (opposite) = S1 + S2, Time = Total length / Relative speed
questions.trap_type: Using relative speed as difference (90-60=30) because one is 'slower'

questions.question: Two trains of equal length are running on parallel lines in the same direction at 46 km/hr and 36 km/hr. The faster train passes the slower train in 36 seconds. The length of each train is:
questions.explanation: Relative speed = 46 - 36 = 10 km/hr = 10 × 5/18 = 25/9 m/s. Let length of each train = x. Total distance = 2x. Time = 36 sec. So 2x = (25/9) × 36 = 100. x = 50 m.
questions.shortcut: Relative speed = 10 km/hr = 25/9 m/s. Distance covered = 25/9 × 36 = 100 m = 2×length. Length = 50 m.
questions.formula_used: Relative speed (same direction) = S1 - S2, Total distance = sum of lengths
questions.trap_type: Setting distance = x (one train length) instead of 2x (both trains)

questions.question: A car travelling at 5/7 of its actual speed covers 42 km in 1 hour 40 minutes 48 seconds. Find the actual speed of the car.
questions.shortcut: Time = 1 + 100/60 + 48/3600 = 6048/3600 hours. Speed = 42/time = 42×3600/6048 = 25 km/hr (reduced speed). Actual = 25 × 7/5 = 35 km/hr.
questions.trap_type: Stopping at 25 km/hr (the reduced speed) instead of finding actual speed

questions.question: Excluding stoppages, the speed of a bus is 54 kmph and including stoppages, it is 45 kmph. For how many minutes does the bus stop per hour?
questions.explanation: Due to stoppages, bus covers 9 km less per hour (54-45=9). Time to cover 9 km at 54 kmph = 9/54 hours = 1/6 hours = 10 minutes.

questions.question: A man in a train travelling at 50 kmph counts 21 telephone posts in one minute. If they are known to be 50 metres apart, then at what speed is the train travelling?
questions.explanation: 21 posts means 20 gaps. Distance = 20 × 50 = 1000 m = 1 km. Time = 1 minute = 1/60 hours. Speed = 1 / (1/60) = 60 kmph.
questions.shortcut: 21 posts = 20 intervals = 1000 m. In 1 min = 1 km per minute = 60 km/hr.
questions.trap_type: Counting 21 gaps instead of 20 (getting 21×50=1050m, speed=63 kmph, option D)

questions.question: A farmer travelled a distance of 61 km in 9 hours. He travelled partly on foot at 4 km/hr and partly on bicycle at 9 km/hr. The distance travelled on foot is:
questions.explanation: Let distance on foot = x km. Distance on bicycle = (61-x) km. Time = x/4 + (61-x)/9 = 9. Multiply by 36: 9x + 4(61-x) = 324. 9x + 244 - 4x = 324. 5x = 80. x = 16 km.
questions.shortcut: Alligation method: Average speed = 61/9 = 6.78 km/hr. Ratio of times = (9-6.78):(6.78-4) = 2.22:2.78 = 4:5 (approx). Distance ratio = 4×4 : 5×9 = 16:45. Total = 61. x = 61 × 16/61 = 16.
questions.trap_type: Assuming equal time for both modes and splitting distance proportionally

questions.question: A train 800 metres long is running at a speed of 78 km/hr. If it crosses a tunnel in 1 minute, then the length of the tunnel (in meters) is:
questions.explanation: Speed = 78 km/hr = 78 × 5/18 = 65/3 m/s. Time = 1 minute = 60 seconds. Distance = (65/3) × 60 = 1300 m. This distance = train length + tunnel length. Tunnel length = 1300 - 800 = 500 m.
questions.shortcut: Distance = speed × time = (78×5/18) × 60 = 1300 m. Tunnel = 1300 - 800 = 500 m.
questions.formula_used: Distance = Speed × Time, Total distance = Train + Tunnel
questions.trap_type: Using only tunnel length = distance (getting 1300, not in options) or forgetting unit conversion

questions.question: A man walks at 5 kmph and misses a train by 7 minutes. If he walks at 6 kmph, he reaches the station 5 minutes before the train arrives. The distance to the station is:
questions.explanation: Let distance = x km. Time difference = 7 + 5 = 12 minutes = 12/60 = 1/5 hours. x/5 - x/6 = 1/5. (6x - 5x)/30 = 1/5. x/30 = 1/5. x = 6 km.
questions.shortcut: Time difference = 12 min = 0.2 hr. x(1/5 - 1/6) = 0.2. x/30 = 0.2. x = 6.
questions.formula_used: Time = Distance/Speed, difference in times = constant
questions.trap_type: Using only 7 minutes or only 5 minutes instead of total 12 minutes difference
